rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Component type contracts should reference ports through valid variables or outputs, not subcomponent ports directly.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structural integrity when inserting AGREE annexes by aligning with component type or implementation scope.
Use only AADL-defined identifiers and types in AGREE expressions to avoid unresolved reference errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be declared in appropriate AADL scopes based on their port and binding context.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or referencing subcomponent ports directly in type contracts.
Do not assume AADL property names (e.g., Deadline, Period) are automatically available as AGREE variables.
